text: Management has articulated a measured approach to sovereign and macroeconomic risk exposure. According to market chatter on October 21, 2025, Bank of America joined JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs in hesitating to finalize a proposed $20 billion loan to Argentina absent guarantee structures, reflecting prudent credit risk discipline. Additionally, CEO Brian Moynihan stated at the American Bankers Association's annual convention that the bank maintains an established playbook for government shutdowns, including fee and payment waivers plus loan deferrals and forbearance programs.
